an5.159:1.2: that on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Kosambī at Ghosita’s monastery.
an5.159:1.3: Now at that time Ven. Udāyin was sitting surrounded by a large assembly of householders, teaching the Dhamma.
an5.159:1.4: Ven. Ānanda saw Ven. Udāyin sitting surrounded by a large assembly of householders, teaching the Dhamma, and on seeing him went to the Blessed One.
an5.159:1.5: On arrival, he bowed down to the Blessed One and sat to one side. 9As he was sitting there he said to the Blessed One:
an5.159:1.6: “Ven. Udāyin, lord, is sitting surrounded by a large assembly of householders, teaching the Dhamma.”
an5.159:2.1: “It’s not easy to teach the Dhamma to others, Ānanda.
an5.159:2.2: The Dhamma should be taught to others only when five qualities are established within the person teaching.
an5.159:2.4: “The Dhamma should be taught with the thought, ‘I will speak step-by-step.’
an5.159:2.5: “The Dhamma should be taught with the thought, ‘I will speak explaining the sequence (of cause & effect).’
an5.159:2.6: “The Dhamma should be taught with the thought, ‘I will speak out of compassion.’
an5.159:2.7: “The Dhamma should be taught with the thought, ‘I will speak not for the purpose of material reward.’
an5.159:2.8: “The Dhamma should be taught with the thought, ‘I will speak without hurting myself or others.’1
an5.159:2.9: “It’s not easy to teach the Dhamma to others, Ānanda.
an5.159:2.10: The Dhamma should be taught to others only when these five qualities are established within the person teaching.”
